scope: AgenC contract tests for personality prompt integration and one-shot personality migration behavior.

requiredBehaviors: Model instruction resolution returns the original base instructions when the model has no personality template metadata.
Supplying a configured personality does not mutate or replace base instructions for models without personality templates.
edgeCases: The base instruction string remains byte-for-byte unchanged after resolving friendly personality on an unsupported model.

requiredBehaviors: An override-shaped model without template metadata returns override instructions directly.
Friendly personality cannot inject template text when the model metadata has no personality template.
edgeCases: The override text is returned exactly rather than being prefixed or suffixed with a blank personality section.

requiredBehaviors: A read-only text turn with no resolved personality sends no personality developer update message.
The assertion inspects the actual provider request emitted by AgenC's turn loop.
edgeCases: A model that supports personality templates still emits no update when the turn personality is absent.

requiredBehaviors: A configured friendly personality is baked into the first request's system instructions.
No personality developer update message is emitted on the first turn when config already supplies the personality.
edgeCases: The provider request contains friendly template text in the system message and zero personality update developer messages.

requiredBehaviors: Configured personality none removes personality template text from system instructions.
Configured personality none emits no personality developer update message.
edgeCases: The provider request contains neither friendly nor pragmatic local template text and no raw personality placeholder.

requiredBehaviors: A startup with prior sessions and no current config file applies the one-shot pragmatic personality migration.
The first turn after bootstrap sends pragmatic template text to the provider.
edgeCases: The prior recorded session is under the selected provider so migration is eligible before the turn context is constructed.

requiredBehaviors: A follow-up turn that changes personality to friendly injects a developer personality update before the user message.
The update contains the personality marker, preamble, and friendly template text.
edgeCases: The first turn has no personality so the second turn must compare against an existing previous turn baseline.

requiredBehaviors: A follow-up turn with the same pragmatic personality does not inject a personality developer update.
The comparison uses the previous turn state recorded by the live AgenC turn loop.
edgeCases: Two consecutive turns with pragmatic personality produce no second-turn personality marker.

requiredBehaviors: An active recorded session with no explicit personality applies the pragmatic migration.
The migration writes the marker and persists pragmatic personality to current config.
edgeCases: The marker file contents are exactly v1 newline after an applied migration.

requiredBehaviors: An archived recorded session also makes the no-personality migration eligible.
Archived-session migration persists pragmatic personality.
edgeCases: The active-session list can be empty while the archived-session list still triggers the migration.

requiredBehaviors: An existing migration marker short-circuits migration before config writes.
No current config file is created when marker short-circuits.
edgeCases: The existing marker contents remain exactly v1 newline.

requiredBehaviors: An explicit configured personality skips the pragmatic migration even when sessions exist.
The explicit personality value is preserved.
edgeCases: The skip path still writes the migration marker with exact v1 newline contents.

requiredBehaviors: No active or archived recorded sessions skips the pragmatic migration.
The no-session skip writes only the migration marker.
edgeCases: No current config file is created on the no-session path.

requiredBehaviors: Legacy JSON config is migrated before the personality edit is applied.
Existing config fields are preserved alongside pragmatic personality in current TOML config.
edgeCases: A backup of the legacy JSON config is created while config.toml receives the migrated value.
